data.title: Euro stablecoins gain backing as Bundesbank warns on dollar
data.description:
Bundesbank President Nagel backs euro stablecoins as ECB advances a digital euro; MiCA rules aim to curb dollarization risks and bolster sovereignty.
source: https://coincu.com/news/euro-stablecoins-gain-backing-as-bundesbank-warns-on-dollar/

data.title: Bitcoin's Accumulation Phase May Extend to 2027, Analyst Suggests
data.description: Bitcoin's on-chain Entity Adjusted Liveliness (EAL) indicator is showing signs of a shift from selling to accumulation. According to NS3.AI, analyst Axel Adler Jr. notes that the EAL has dropped below significant moving averages, indicating a resistance level and reflecting previous accumulation patterns observed in 2020 and 2022-2024. If this trend continues, Bitcoin's current accumulation phase could persist until mid-2027.

data.title: BRICS Launches New Payment System to Bypass US Dollar
data.description: BRICS countries have introduced a new payment system, DCMS, based in Brazil, aimed at facilitating cross-border trade settlements without relying on the US dollar. According to NS3.AI, this system utilizes Brazil's Pix instant transfer technology and operates on a decentralized blockchain network. It connects major member states and integrates local currencies and digital national currencies. Meanwhile, investor sentiment towards the dollar is becoming increasingly negative, supporting BRICS' initiative to minimize dependence on traditional dollar-based systems.

data.title: Toshiba Considers Stake Reduction in Elevator Unit Amid Interest from Kone Oyj
data.description:
Toshiba is contemplating a reduction in its stake in its elevator division, responding to interest from Finland's Kone Oyj. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that Toshiba's move comes as part of a strategic review of its business operations. The Japanese conglomerate is evaluating various options to optimize its portfolio and enhance shareholder value.

Kone Oyj, a prominent player in the elevator and escalator industry, has expressed interest in Toshiba's elevator unit, which is known for its advanced technology and strong market presence. The potential stake reduction aligns with Toshiba's broader efforts to streamline its operations and focus on core areas.

The decision to consider a stake reduction is part of Toshiba's ongoing strategy to adapt to changing market dynamics and improve its financial performance. The company has been undergoing significant restructuring in recent years, aiming to strengthen its competitive position in the global market.

Toshiba's elevator unit has been a key contributor to its business, offering innovative solutions and maintaining a solid reputation in the industry. The interest from Kone Oyj underscores the unit's value and potential for growth.

As Toshiba explores its options, the company remains committed to maximizing shareholder returns and ensuring sustainable growth. The outcome of this strategic review could have implications for the future direction of Toshiba's business operations.

data.title: Market news: Charles Schwab, with $12 trillion in assets under management, increased its holdings in Strategy by over 91,000 shares, bringing its total holdings to $168 million.
data.description: BitcoinTreasuries.NET reported on the X platform that Charles Schwab, with $12 trillion in assets under management, increased its holdings of Strategy (MSTR) stock by 91,859 shares. Its total holdings now reach 1.27 million shares, valued at $168 million.

data.title: Gen Alpha's Economic Influence Set to Rise
data.description: Gen Alpha, comprising 25% of the global population, is on the verge of becoming a significant economic force. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this generation on the global economy. As Gen Alpha matures, their influence on consumer trends, technological advancements, and economic policies is expected to grow substantially. Experts suggest that businesses and policymakers should prepare for the shifts this generation will bring, as their preferences and behaviors will shape future markets. The rise of Gen Alpha is anticipated to redefine economic landscapes, prompting strategic adaptations across various sectors.

data.title: Telegram Integrates with OpenClaw in Latest Beta Update
data.description: Crypto Jargon posted on X. Telegram has announced its integration with OpenClaw in its latest beta update, enhancing security and connectivity for users. This development marks a significant step in improving the platform's functionality and user experience. The integration aims to provide a more seamless and secure communication environment, aligning with Telegram's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings.

data.title: Trader Sees Significant Gains with VVV Tokens
data.description: A trader known as @changefomo has made a substantial investment in VVV tokens, committing over $700,000. According to NS3.AI, the current value of these holdings has risen to nearly $1 million, resulting in total profits of $430,000 on the FOMO platform. This case underscores a notable return on investment achieved in a relatively brief timeframe.

data.title: Nyrstar Completes First Shipment of Antimony from Australia
data.description: Nyrstar, a company under the Trafigura Group, has successfully completed its first shipment of antimony from Port Pirie, Australia. According to Jin10, this initial batch is set to be utilized by a domestic manufacturer on Australia's east coast. Future shipments are planned for export to clients in Europe, Asia, or the United States.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF trimmed as Harvard adds ETHA in Q4 filing
data.description:
Filing data show Harvard Management Company, Bitcoin ETF, Ethereum ETF: HMC cut IBIT ~21% and opened ~$86. 8M in ETHA, a shift analysts cite as diversification.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-etf-trimmed-as-harvard-adds-etha-in-q4-filing/

data.title: Agant, a stablecoin issuer specializing in British pounds, predicts that UK crypto legislation may come into effect in 2027.
data.description: Andrew MacKenzie, CEO of Agant, a stablecoin issuer specializing in sterling-denominated cryptocurrencies, stated that while the UK's crypto regulatory framework is on the right track, its progress is insufficient to support the country's ambition to become a global digital asset hub. MacKenzie believes the UK government has repeatedly pledged to position London as a global center for crypto and digital asset activity. However, legislation comprehensively covering stablecoins and broader crypto activities is expected to receive parliamentary approval later this year, with full implementation potentially not until 2027. Agant plans to issue a stablecoin called GBPA, fully backed 1:1 by the pound sterling. The company has already registered with the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), indicating its intention to target institutional investors rather than the retail cryptocurrency market. Agant has positioned GBPA as infrastructure for institutional payments, settlements, and tokenized assets. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bundesbank President: Euro stablecoins and CBDCs help enhance the independence of the European payment system
data.description: Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel supports the introduction of a euro-denominated central bank digital currency (CBDC) and a euro stablecoin for payments. Nagel pointed out that EU officials are working to advance the introduction of a retail CBDC, and that a euro stablecoin would help make Europe more independent in its payment systems and solutions, and reduce cross-border payment costs for individuals and businesses. Nagel believes that a CBDC would allow financial institutions to use central bank currency for programmable payments. However, Nagel warned that if dollar-denominated stablecoins gain a significantly larger market share than euro-denominated stablecoins, domestic monetary policy could be severely damaged, and European sovereignty could be weakened. Currently, the US has signed legislation establishing a framework for payment-based stablecoins, and the US Senate is discussing the CLARITY Act. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: A trader spent over $700,000 to buy VVV and made a total profit of $430,000.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, trader @changefomo spent over $700,000 to buy VVV tokens, and his current holdings are worth nearly $1 million. His total profit on the FOMO platform has reached $430,000.

data.title: India's Role as a Global Capability Center Expands
data.description: India is increasingly becoming a significant hub for global capability centers, highlighting its expanding influence in the global business landscape. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the country's growing importance in this sector. These centers, which provide a range of services from IT support to financial analysis, are pivotal in the operations of multinational companies. India's skilled workforce and cost-effective solutions make it an attractive destination for these centers. As more companies look to optimize their operations, India's role is expected to grow further, solidifying its position as a key player in the global market.

data.title: Crypto Trader 0x58bro Secures $7 Million in Profits from Short Positions
data.description: A crypto trader operating under the pseudonym 0x58bro has achieved $7 million in floating profits through strategic short positions. According to NS3.AI, the trader's most substantial earnings come from shorting Ethereum (ETH) and Enarx (ENA), with profits amounting to $3.7 million and $1.45 million, respectively. Despite these impressive gains, 0x58bro maintains a modest following of 1,300 on the X social media platform.

data.title: Kalshi's February Trading Volume Shows Significant Growth
data.description: Kalshi has recorded over 34.8 million trades as of February 14, with an average daily trading volume of approximately 2.5 million, according to PANews. In comparison, January saw about 54.5 million trades, indicating that February's trading volume has already reached around 64% of the previous month's total. If the current pace continues, Kalshi's monthly trading volume is on track to set a new record, potentially surpassing 69.6 million trades.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Greece to Attend Peace Committee Meeting as Observer in Washington
data.description: Greece has announced its participation as an observer in the upcoming Peace Committee meeting in Washington, marking the second phase of the Gaza ceasefire process. According to Jin10, this development comes as international efforts continue to stabilize the region and promote peace. The meeting aims to address ongoing tensions and explore solutions for lasting peace in Gaza. Greece's involvement highlights its commitment to supporting diplomatic initiatives and contributing to regional stability.

data.title: A whale with only 1,300 followers, 0x58bro, made a paper profit of $7 million by shorting assets such as ETH and ENA.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, trader 0x58bro (0x58b...f35) currently holds short positions with a floating profit of $7 million. His main profits come from shorting ETH (with a floating profit of $3.7 million) and ENA (with a floating profit of $1.45 million). It's worth noting that this trader only has 1,300 followers on the X platform.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake, the American fast-food chain, has seen a significant increase in sales by adopting a Bitcoin payment and reserve strategy.
data.description: Steak 'n Shake, the American fast-food chain, announced on the X platform that it has seen a significant increase in sales due to its adoption of a BTC payment and reserve strategy. The company noted that BTC adoption has not only boosted brand awareness but also attracted a large number of new customers. Steak 'n Shake has now incorporated BTC into its balance sheet and plans to further expand the use of cryptocurrency in its business. (Coindesk)

data.title: Polygon's daily transaction fee revenue surpassed Ethereum's yesterday.
data.description: Cointelegraph reported on the X platform that, according to Token Terminal data, Polygon's daily transaction fees exceeded $300,000, surpassing Ethereum.
